evidence: The paper uses Random Forest with SHAP importance analysis: "the SHAP-beeswarm plot highlighted the significant roles of baseline MDS-UPDRS III score, albumin, BMI, and mean striatum volume." No explicit mention of Gini/MDI/variance importance. Primary findings established via multinomial logistic regression with covariate adjustment: "The analysis revealed that baseline MDS-UPDRS III score, albumin, BMI (per 5-unit increase), and DaTscan striatum were significantly associated with the trajectories" (all p<0.05). Machine learning presented as confirmatory: "Machine learning analysis confirmed the predictive importance of these factors, with the Random Forest model achieving an AUC of 0.950." Features include mixed types (continuous, binary, multi-category scales) with scale mismatch (raw measurements vs. ordinal scores).
